summary: Solid contract with thorough coverage of all sprint features: boss vulnerability windows per world, boss HP ranges per world, arena structural checks, level path fixes, and architectural code-review criteria. Criteria are specific, quantitative, and testable via the Sprint 14 validator API. Minor redundancy between per-world and aggregate criteria is acceptable for diagnostic granularity. A few suggestions noted below but nothing blocking acceptance.

criteriaReview.notes: Fully subsumed by C-02 (which checks all World 1 including 1-1), but acceptable as a focused check on the specifically-called-out broken boss from the user prompt. Keeps it for traceability.
Clear threshold, testable via console. Good.
Clear threshold, testable via console. Good.
Clear threshold, testable via console. Good.
Clear threshold, testable via console. Good.
Clear threshold, testable via console. Good.
Aggregate of C-02 through C-06. Redundant but useful as a single summary check. Keeps the evaluator honest that nothing was skipped.
HP range [5,7] for World 1. Clear, testable.
HP range [6,8] for World 2. Clear, testable.
HP range [7,9] for World 3. Clear, testable.
HP range [7,11] for World 4. Clear, testable.
Exact HP = 19 for Architect. Clear, testable.
Aggregate HP check. Same redundancy pattern as C-07. Acceptable.
Platforms >= 2 per arena. Directly from user prompt requirement. Good.
Arena entry validation. Directly from user prompt. Good.
Arena enclosure validation. Good.
Arena minimum width >= 15 tiles. Directly from user prompt. Good.

Code-review method is acceptable for boss clamping. A runtime test would be stronger (spawn boss, advance frames, check x position) but code-review is pragmatic given the difficulty of automating boss movement in a headless context.
Code-review method is acceptable. Same reasoning as C-18 — triggering player death programmatically to verify boss reset is complex to automate.
Key criterion — Stage 1-2 was explicitly called out as broken/impassable. Validator confirms traversability. Good.
Code-review for wall-jump section widening. Threshold of 4-tile gaps and intermediate platforms is specific enough. Good.
Aggregate path validation — all 13 stages. Critical safety net. Good.
Architectural criterion ensuring per-world config lookup rather than per-boss hardcoding. Good for maintainability and correctness. Frame values specified.
Aggregate arena check (52 sub-checks). Redundant with C-14 through C-17 but provides a single summary number. Acceptable.
Aggregate boss difficulty check (26 sub-checks). Redundant with C-07 + C-13 but harmless.
FPS regression check. Threshold of >= 55 fps is reasonable (allows some margin from target 60). Good.
Validator structural regression — ensures validator still produces complete results after game data changes. Good.
World map regression. 13 nodes across 5 worlds. Good.

testMethod 'manual' may be harder to automate. Suggest the evaluator use Playwright keyboard simulation (press 'D', check for overlay elements) if available. Acceptable as-is since the debug overlay is a Sprint 14 feature and regression testing it is prudent.

Smoke test for core gameplay systems. Threshold is somewhat vague ('player moves, jumps, attacks normally') but checking for existence of Player/Physics/Input/Camera/Renderer objects is a reasonable minimum bar. Acceptable.

coverageAnalysis.sprintPlanFeature: F25: Difficulty Curve Rebalancing (level fixes + boss tuning)
coveredAreas: Boss vulnerability windows per world (C-01 through C-07)
Boss HP ranges per world (C-08 through C-13)
Boss arena structural integrity: platforms, entry, enclosure, width (C-14 through C-17)
Boss behavioral correctness: clamping, reset on death (C-18, C-19)
Stage 1-2 layout fix and path validation (C-20, C-21)
Performance and structural regression (C-26 through C-30)
gaps: No explicit criterion for Stage 1-1 layout/path fix — though Stage 1-1's issue was the boss vulnerability window (covered by C-01/C-02) rather than path traversability, so this is acceptable.

User prompt mentions 'Recovery after jump landing should be obvious and generous' for Elder Shroomba — this qualitative aspect is only indirectly tested via the 3.0s vulnerability window duration. Acceptable since the 3.0s window is the quantified version of 'generous'.

No criterion verifies that bosses can be defeated using only mechanics introduced up to that point (user prompt requirement) — this is very difficult to automate and is arguably Sprint 16 scope (mechanics gating is listed in outOfScope). Acceptable omission.

outOfScopeReview.notes: Enemy density, hazard percentage, platform width, and mechanics gating are properly deferred to Sprint 16 as stated in the sprint plan.

regressionSprintsReview.notes: All 14 prior sprints listed. Sprint 15 depends on 14 which depends on 13, etc. Full regression chain is covered.

suggestions: Consider converting C-29 from 'manual' to 'console' testMethod with steps like: simulate D keypress via window.dispatchEvent, then check for overlay DOM/canvas state. Would make fully automated evaluation easier.
C-30 could be strengthened by checking specific method existence (e.g., typeof Player.prototype.move === 'function') rather than just 'verify Player object exists'. Minor improvement.

The 5 aggregate criteria (C-07, C-13, C-22, C-24, C-25) overlap heavily with their per-world counterparts. Not a problem — but the evaluator should test the per-world criteria first and only check aggregates as confirmation, to avoid masking which world specifically failed.
